title: Ervum floridanum
taxonomy: Ervum floridanum is a legume plant, specifically belonging to the family Fabaceae. It is also classified as being in the genus Ervum, and the subfamily Faboideae. As a legume, it falls under the order Fabales. The species is further categorized as being in the kingdom Plantae. Within the family Fabaceae, Ervum floridanum is also known to be closely related to other similar legume species.

intro: Ervum floridanum, also known as Florida bean or wild bean, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the legume family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Ervum floridanum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Ervum floridanum is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and fever.
